QF.id: VI Debt Instruments and Government Securities_20

question.text: What is the maturity period of Dated Securities?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Dated Securities have a maturity period of more than one year, distinguishing them from Treasury Bills which are up to one year. **The Trap:** Exactly 5 years is a common tenor but dated securities can have various maturities beyond one year. **Cross-Exam Context:** Classification of government securities by maturity is tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Dated G-Secs can have maturities ranging from a few years to 40 years — they are the backbone of the government bond market.
